reasoning: Chad officially approved Starlink's license in November 2024 and the formal operating agreement was signed on March 13, 2025. At that time, the Chadian government announced that the service would be available nationwide "by the end of 2025." While Starlink often operationalizes quickly after final regulatory clearance (e.g., within 3-4 months in some African countries, which would suggest a June or July launch), the explicit "by the end of 2025" timeframe introduces the likelihood of a Q3 or Q4 rollout. As of the May 2025 knowledge cutoff, the service has not yet gone live. With only 1.5 months remaining until the July 1, 2025 resolution date, the chances of Starlink moving from the license-signing phase to full commercial availability (and updating its map to "Available") are roughly a coin toss, leaning slightly toward a launch occurring later in the year.
